steps.title: Identify the present-tense signal
steps.content: The passage opens with Beal's 1879 experiment, then immediately signals ongoing research with “researchers are conducting” periodic checks and describes a retrieval from as recently as 2021. This present-tense framing anchors the passage's purpose.

steps.title: Match to the main purpose
steps.content:
Choice A directly describes what the passage does: frame a historical experiment as an ongoing investigation with recent findings.

  • B invents a plant-removal purpose (absent)
  • C claims two separate experiments are compared (only one is described)
  • D treats the passage as explaining preservation difficulties (it simply reports outcomes)
.

That matches choice A — A is correct.
